name: Bangkok
tagline: World-Class City Living at a Fraction of the Price — Retire in Bangkok, Thailand
description: Bangkok delivers the energy, cuisine, and infrastructure of a global megacity at Southeast Asian prices. With its legendary street food, ultra-modern malls, excellent hospitals, and a massive international expat scene, Bangkok offers FIRE retirees a vibrant urban lifestyle that punches far above its cost.

quickFacts.visa: 60-day tourist visa on arrival; Long-Term Resident (LTR) visa for retirees with $80K+ income or $1M+ assets; Thai Elite visa ($15K–$30K) for 5–20 year stays
quickFacts.safetyRating: Safe — standard big-city precautions advised; low violent crime
quickFacts.nearestAirport: Suvarnabhumi (BKK) and Don Mueang (DMK) — major international hub with flights worldwide
quickFacts.climate: Tropical wet with hot season (Mar–May), rainy season (Jun–Oct), and cool season (Nov–Feb)

housing.description: Studio or small 1-bedroom condo near BTS/MRT with pool and gym in areas like On Nut or Bang Na

dining.description: Street food staples like pad krapow and som tam daily; occasional mall food courts

healthcare.description: Basic private insurance covering outpatient visits; access to hospitals like Phyathai or Saint Louis

transportation.description: BTS/MRT monthly pass supplemented by occasional Grab rides

entertainment.description: Weekend markets, temple visits, rooftop happy hours, free cultural events

1M.lifestyle: You live in a compact but modern condo steps from the Skytrain, eating incredible street food for a dollar a plate and exploring a new neighborhood every weekend. Bangkok at $1M is tight but thrilling — you trade space for access to one of the world's most dynamic cities.

housing.description: Well-appointed 1-bedroom or 2-bedroom condo in Thong Lo, Ekkamai, or Ari with full facilities

dining.description: Regular restaurant dining across Thai, Japanese, and international cuisines; occasional hotel brunches

healthcare.description: Comprehensive private insurance with Bumrungrad or Bangkok Hospital access

transportation.description: BTS/MRT plus regular Grab rides and occasional taxi trips

entertainment.description: Premium gym, cinema, nightlife in Thong Lo, weekend trips to Hua Hin or Koh Samet

2M.lifestyle: At $2M, Bangkok opens up beautifully. You settle into the trendy Thong Lo area with its Japanese restaurants, craft cocktail bars, and leafy sois. Weekend beach escapes are easy, healthcare is world-class, and you can dine out every single night at excellent restaurants without worrying about your budget.

housing.description: Spacious 2-bedroom luxury condo in Sukhumvit, Sathorn, or Riverside with skyline views and premium amenities

dining.description: Fine dining at Michelin-starred restaurants, omakase bars, rooftop restaurants, and hotel dining rooms

healthcare.description: Premium international insurance; executive health packages at Bumrungrad; dental and vision included

transportation.description: Combination of BTS, Grab, and hired car; occasional domestic flights to islands and Chiang Mai

entertainment.description: Members-only clubs, premium spa treatments, Muay Thai training, sailing club, frequent domestic travel

3M.lifestyle: With $3M, you experience Bangkok's glamorous side. You live in a stunning high-rise with Chao Phraya River views, dine at Gaggan alumni restaurants, and have a social life that revolves around rooftop bars and private events. Bangkok's Michelin scene is your playground, and island-hopping weekends are routine.

housing.description: Penthouse condo or luxury serviced residence in prime Sathorn or Sukhumvit; alternatively a large house in upscale Nichada Thani

dining.description: Personal chef for weekday meals; Michelin restaurants and exclusive chef's table experiences on weekends

healthcare.description: VIP hospital packages at Bumrungrad with personal health coordinator; global evacuation coverage

transportation.description: Leased luxury sedan with part-time driver; domestic first-class flights; helicopter transfers to resorts

entertainment.description: Royal Bangkok Sports Club membership, art gallery openings, yacht charters to nearby islands, international travel

domesticHelp.description: Live-in maid, part-time cook, and weekly deep-cleaning service

luxuryExtras.description: Bespoke suits from Bangkok tailors, premium wellness retreats, luxury hotel staycations, fine wine collection

5M.lifestyle: At $5M in Bangkok, you live like a titan of industry. Penthouse living with a personal driver, regular chef's table dinners, and weekends on private yachts to the Gulf islands. Bangkok's luxury scene is world-class and remarkably accessible — you'd need $15M+ for this lifestyle in New York or London.

housing.description: Ultra-luxury penthouse on the Chao Phraya or sprawling mansion in Nichada Thani with private pool and gardens; optional second residence on a Thai island

dining.description: Private chef team, imported delicacies, exclusive supper clubs, and Michelin dining as a weekly routine

healthcare.description: Concierge medical service with 24/7 access to top specialists; annual comprehensive screenings; global coverage

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle fleet, full-time chauffeur, first-class international flights, private jet charters for regional travel

entertainment.description: Elite club memberships, private island retreats, art collecting, philanthropic galas, frequent international luxury travel

domesticHelp.description: Full household staff: chef, housekeeper, nanny/assistant, driver, gardener, and security

luxuryExtras.description: Art patronage, private island weekends, bespoke fashion, investment in Bangkok real estate, charitable foundations

10M.lifestyle: With $10M in Bangkok, you command a lifestyle that most people associate with old-money dynasties. Your penthouse overlooks the river, your staff anticipates every need, and you move between Bangkok high society, island retreats, and international travel with effortless grace. Bangkok is one of the best cities on earth to be wealthy in.
